title: SectionAnalyses Object
description: Provides access to any section analyses results in the design and supports the ability to create new sections.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a new SectionAnalysisInput object to use when creating a new Section Analysis. A SectionAnalysisInput object is the API equivalent of the command dialog that contains the inputs to create a section analysis. Use this object to define the settings you need and then pass this into the add method to create the section analysis.

methods.name: item
methods.description: A method that returns the specified SectionAnalysis object using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: A method that returns the specified SectionAnalysis object using the name of the analysis as displayed in the browser.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of SectionAnalysis objects in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: SectionAnalyses.add Method
add.return_description: Returns the new SectionAnalysis object if successful.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A SectionAnalysisInput object that defines how the section analysis should be created. Use the createInput method to create a new SectionAnalysisInput object.

classType.title: SectionAnalyses.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: SectionAnalyses.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a new SectionAnalysisInput object to use when creating a new Section Analysis. A SectionAnalysisInput object is the API equivalent of the command dialog that contains the inputs to create a section analysis. Use this object to define the settings you need and then pass this into the add method to create the section analysis.
createInput.return_description: Returns a SectionAnalysisInput object if successful.

parameters.name: cutPlaneEntity
parameters.description: The planar entity used to define the cut plane and can be either a planar BRepFace or a ConstructionPlane object.

parameters.name: distance
parameters.description: The offset distance of the section from the cut plane. A positive value will offset in the positive normal direction of the cut plane entity. The value is in centimeters. This value is used to create a transformation matrix that defines the specified offset.

item.title: SectionAnalyses.item Method
item.description: A method that returns the specified SectionAnalysis object using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: SectionAnalyses.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: A method that returns the specified SectionAnalysis object using the name of the analysis as displayed in the browser.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid name was specified.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the SectionAnalysis object as displayed in the browser.

count.title: SectionAnalyses.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of SectionAnalysis objects in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sectionAnalyses_var.count

isValid.title: SectionAnalyses.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sectionAnalyses_var.isValid

objectType.title: SectionAnalyses.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sectionAnalyses_var.objectType
